content:
type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py (Image.save method, ~line 2469-2591)
summary: Adding keyword-only dry_run=False param to save(); when True, writes go to an in-memory buffer instead of opening/creating the real file, and the real file is never opened/created/removed. I will add it near the top of save() signature and guard the open_fp file-opening block; will keep diff minimal so your backup logic (likely around the os.path.exists/created check) can coexist - happy to rebase around whichever of us lands first

content:
type: ANSWER
files: src/PIL/Image.py (Image.save method)
summary: Landed dry_run: changed the old 'if open_fp:' block to 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: created = not os.path.exists(filename); <your backup copy can go here>; if params.get(append)... '. Also guarded the exception/finally close calls with 'and not dry_run'. Please put your backup copy inside the elif open_fp branch (after computing created, before builtins.open) - that way it's naturally skipped during dry_run too, which is correct since dry_run must not touch disk at all. Shout if you hit a conflict applying your diff.
